2603.11598.title: Survival Meets Classification: A Novel Framework for Early Risk Prediction Models of Chronic Diseases
2603.11598.paper_summary: Chronic diseases are long-lasting conditions that require lifelong medical attention. Using big EMR data, we have developed early disease risk prediction models for five common chronic diseases: diabetes, hypertension, CKD, COPD, and chronic ischemic heart disease. In this study, we present a novel approach for disease risk models by integrating survival analysis with classification techniques. Traditional models for predicting the risk of chronic diseases predominantly focus on either survival analysis or classification independently. In this paper, we show survival analysis methods can be re-engineered to enable them to do classification efficiently and effectively, thereby making them a comprehensive tool for developing disease risk surveillance models. The results of our experiments on real-world big EMR data show that the performance of survival models in terms of accuracy, F1 score, and AUROC is comparable to or better than that of prior state-of-the-art models like LightGBM and XGBoost. Lastly, the proposed survival models use a novel methodology to generate explanations, which have been clinically validated by a panel of three expert physicians.
2603.11598.paper_summary_zh: 慢性疾病是需要終身醫療關注的長期疾病。利用大規模電子病歷數據，我們為五種常見的慢性疾病開發了早期疾病風險預測模型：糖尿病、高血壓、慢性腎病、慢性阻塞性肺病和慢性缺血性心臟病。在這項研究中，我們提出了一種通過將生存分析與分類技術相結合來構建疾病風險模型的新方法。傳統的慢性疾病風險預測模型主要獨立關注生存分析或分類。本文中，我們展示了生存分析方法可以重新設計，使其能夠高效且有效地進行分類，從而使其成為開發疾病風險監測模型的綜合工具。我們在真實世界的大規模電子病歷數據上的實驗結果顯示，生存模型在準確性、F1分數和AUROC方面的表現與先前的最先進模型如LightGBM和XGBoost相當或更好。最後，所提出的生存模型使用了一種新方法來生成解釋，這些解釋已由三位專家醫生進行臨床驗證。
2603.11598.author: Shaheer Ahmad Khan et.al.
2603.11598.authors: Shaheer Ahmad Khan, Muhammad Usamah Shahid, Muddassar Farooq
